module1.clean_text:
HDJ RE-hospitalization report
The child [PATIENT] aged [AGE] from Chlef and residing in Oran, followed in our department since [DATE] for a primary immunodeficiency type "Wiskott Aldrich syndrome", complicated by dilation of the cylindrical DDB bronchi and chronic respiratory failure stage 1. Admitted on [DATE] for his treatment 51 immunoglobulins
History of illness:
Main diagnosis: Wiskott Aldrich type DIP
Date of diagnosis: [DATE] of diagnosis: 04 years and 9 [AGE] diagnoses: Dilation of the cylindrical bronchi
Chronic respiratory failure stage 1
Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestics
-Male gender
-Repeated respiratory infections
-Several outbreaks of eczema. Clinic
-Anemic syndrome consisting of: mucocutaneous pallor. -Skin hemorrhagic syndrome such as petechiae spread to the face and trunk. -Eczema lesions on the face and lower limbs. Para-clinical:
-Bi cytopenia; anemia, thrombocytopenia
- Assessment of primary immunodeficiency: [DATE] Profound reduction in the expression of the WASp protein results in favor of DIP Wiskott Aldrich syndrome
Immunoglobulin treatment at a rate of 0.8mg1/kg/4h at the SAP each month. Last dose received: [DATE] (0.8g/kg)
Last update made in [DATE] OF HOSPITALIZATIONS
ANAMNESIS [DATE] since the last visit:
Consultation: no
On the respiratory level: wet cough, clear sputum,
Signs of respiratory failure: Clubbing
Nutritionally: good food intake
On the digestive level: Preserved transit
On the ENT level: Nothing to report
Activity: dyspnea
Schooling: 3rd year of primary school
Processing in progress:
Azithromycin 1 day out of [DATE] 25mg/kg/d
Itraconazole 05mg/kg/day: 02 times/day
Pulmicort 1mg: 02 times/day
Nebulization with hypertonic salt serum
Respiratory physiotherapy done by the mother
Well-kept notebooks
Assessments made:
IgG level: 11/18/2025: 10.86g/l
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 16/12/2025
-Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 31kg [-0.018] Height: 130 cm [-0.97DS] BMI: 17.7 kg/m2 Eutrophic Child
-General condition preserved, conscious, T: 37.2
-Skin examination: no cutaneous and mucosal hemorrhagic syndrome, dry skin, multiple scars on the lower limbs
-Pleuropulmonary examination: FR: 38c/min SPo2: 96% Under 2 liters of O2, slight signs of struggle. Thoracic keel deformity, clubbing
. Auscultation of polymorphic rales
-Cardiovascular examination: HR: 98 bpm, Auscultation: no murmur/no added noises
-Examination of the abdomen: flexible transit preserved, no organomegaly
-ENT examination: Presence of dental caries, absence of hemorrhagic bubbles
-Examination of other devices without anomalies
Paraclinical assessment at admission: 03/11/2025:
WBC: 7800 elements/ul Hb: 10.6g/dl VGM: 73.5 Fl Platelet: 100,000 elements/ul (Thrombopenia)
TP: 89.8% TQ: 13.7 sec TCK: 29 sec Fibrinogene: 3.07 g/l
CRP: negative VS: 33mm/h (the 1st hour) 79 mm/h (the 2nd hour)
Residual IgG: 10.86 g/l (11/18/2025)
Support:
-Hospitalization
- Conditioning, peripheral venous route
-Replacement immunotherapy: IV infusion at 0.8g/kg or 25g, to be administered over 4 hours (Intratect)
-Monitoring
-Stomato opinion made: Presence of dental caries, poor oral condition
CAT: Oral hygiene, for extraction the platelet count must be greater than 80,000 elements
The immunoglobulin treatment passed without incident
Same day release
Continue the same treatment: bactrim antibiotic prophylaxis and itraconazole antifungal
Zithromax, Pulmicort
Physiotherapy and saline nebulizations
Hygiene-dietary advice and notebook keeping
Control appointment: 01/06/2026 for immunoglobulin treatment
To collect / To do:
Next immunoglobulin treatment appointment is scheduled for 01/06/2026
Plan an ECBC to recover before the next treatment
Plan a chest CT,
Schedule an abdominopelvic ultrasound
Schedule an echocardiogram
Retrieve the health record
Sibling screening
Department resident: Dr Djoudi Department assistant: Dr Khelil

inconsistencies.message: Female-specific term 'para' found in male patient record

treatment.expanded_text: treatment of nonalcoholic fatty liver disease in children

phenopacket.input:
HDJ RE-hospitalization report
The child Amroun Abdel Aziz, aged 09 from Chlef and residing in Oran, followed in our department since 01/11/2021 for a primary immunodeficiency type "Wiskott Aldrich syndrome", complicated by dilation of the cylindrical DDB bronchi and chronic respiratory failure stage 1. Admitted on 12/16/2025 for his treatment 51 immunoglobulins
History of illness:
Main diagnosis: Wiskott Aldrich type DIP
Date of diagnosis: 12/11/2020
Age of diagnosis: 04 years and 9 months
Associated diagnoses: Dilation of the cylindrical bronchi
Chronic respiratory failure stage 1
Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestics
-Male gender
-Repeated respiratory infections
-Several outbreaks of eczema. Clinic
-Anemic syndrome consisting of: mucocutaneous pallor. -Skin hemorrhagic syndrome such as petechiae spread to the face and trunk. -Eczema lesions on the face and lower limbs. Para-clinical:
-Bi cytopenia; anemia, thrombocytopenia
- Assessment of primary immunodeficiency: 12/29/2020, Profound reduction in the expression of the WASp protein results in favor of DIP Wiskott Aldrich syndrome
Immunoglobulin treatment at a rate of 0.8mg1/kg/4h at the SAP each month. Last dose received: 02/09/2026 (0.8g/kg)
Last update made in May 2025
HISTORY OF HOSPITALIZATIONS
ANAMNESIS 12/16/2025
Events since the last visit:
Consultation: no
On the respiratory level: wet cough, clear sputum,
Signs of respiratory failure: Clubbing
Nutritionally: good food intake
On the digestive level: Preserved transit
On the ENT level: Nothing to report
Activity: dyspnea
Schooling: 3rd year of primary school
Processing in progress:
Azithromycin 1 day out of 02
Bactrim 25mg/kg/d
Itraconazole 05mg/kg/day: 02 times/day
Pulmicort 1mg: 02 times/day
Nebulization with hypertonic salt serum
Respiratory physiotherapy done by the mother
Well-kept notebooks
Assessments made:
IgG level: 11/18/2025: 10.86g/l
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 16/12/2025
-Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 31kg [-0.018] Height: 130 cm [-0.97DS] BMI: 17.7 kg/m2 Eutrophic Child
-General condition preserved, conscious, T: 37.2
-Skin examination: no cutaneous and mucosal hemorrhagic syndrome, dry skin, multiple scars on the lower limbs
-Pleuropulmonary examination: FR: 38c/min SPo2: 96% Under 2 liters of O2, slight signs of struggle. Thoracic keel deformity, clubbing
. Auscultation of polymorphic rales
-Cardiovascular examination: HR: 98 bpm, Auscultation: no murmur/no added noises
-Examination of the abdomen: flexible transit preserved, no organomegaly
-ENT examination: Presence of dental caries, absence of hemorrhagic bubbles
-Examination of other devices without anomalies
Paraclinical assessment at admission: 03/11/2025:
WBC: 7800 elements/ul Hb: 10.6g/dl VGM: 73.5 Fl Platelet: 100,000 elements/ul (Thrombopenia)
TP: 89.8% TQ: 13.7 sec TCK: 29 sec Fibrinogene: 3.07 g/l
CRP: negative VS: 33mm/h (the 1st hour) 79 mm/h (the 2nd hour)
Residual IgG: 10.86 g/l (11/18/2025)
Support:
-Hospitalization
- Conditioning, peripheral venous route
-Replacement immunotherapy: IV infusion at 0.8g/kg or 25g, to be administered over 4 hours (Intratect)
-Monitoring
-Stomato opinion made: Presence of dental caries, poor oral condition
CAT: Oral hygiene, for extraction the platelet count must be greater than 80,000 elements
The immunoglobulin treatment passed without incident
Same day release
Continue the same treatment: bactrim antibiotic prophylaxis and itraconazole antifungal
Zithromax, Pulmicort
Physiotherapy and saline nebulizations
Hygiene-dietary advice and notebook keeping
Control appointment: 01/06/2026 for immunoglobulin treatment
To collect / To do:
Next immunoglobulin treatment appointment is scheduled for 01/06/2026
Plan an ECBC to recover before the next treatment
Plan a chest CT,
Schedule an abdominopelvic ultrasound
Schedule an echocardiogram
Retrieve the health record
Sibling screening
Department resident: Dr Djoudi Department assistant: Dr Khelil
